mechanism_narrative: Sustained higher federal funds rate indicates tighter monetary policy, increasing the cost of capital and discounting future earnings at a higher rate, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where FEDFUNDS is rising, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate, exceeding 0.5 percentage points over three months, signals an accelerating economic downturn and weakening consumer demand, which will lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the average 60d return following triggers is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales combined with strong housing starts indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand, signaling a healthy economic environment that supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: When both RSAFS and HOUST show positive growth, SPX 20d returns are negative in more than 50% of observations, or the interaction term's coefficient is not statistically significant.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of industrial expansion, increasing industrial production reflects strong manufacturing activity and corporate profitability, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the short term (5 trading days).
failure_condition: In identified industrial expansion regimes, periods of rising INDPRO are associated with negative 5d SPX returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in real GDP on a year-over-year basis indicates a recessionary environment, leading to widespread corporate earnings downgrades and sustained negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth rate falls below 0% YoY
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 40% of triggered instances, or the average 60d return following triggers is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in non-farm payrolls indicates a healthy labor market and robust consumer spending capacity, which generally supports corporate revenues and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 55% of periods where PAYEMS is growing, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is persistently above 3% year-over-year and the Federal Reserve is actively raising the federal funds rate, it signals an aggressive monetary tightening cycle to combat inflation, which typically leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL > 3% YoY AND FEDFUNDS is rising
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 30% of triggered instances, or the average 60d return following triggers is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: In periods of strong consumer confidence, robust retail sales growth indicates healthy consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate revenues and leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the very short term (5 trading days).
failure_condition: In identified strong consumer confidence regimes, periods of rising RSAFS are associated with negative 5d SPX returns more than 50% of the time.
